prompts.label: Node.js Hello World
prompts.text:
I'm new to Node.js. Create a minimal project.

- package.json with name and type module (ESM) OR CommonJS — pick one and explain
- index.js that logs Hello from Node
- Commands: npm init, node index.js

Mention Node LTS version briefly.

prompts.label: DOM click counter
prompts.text:
Build a beginner DOM exercise: click counter.

- index.html with a button and a span showing count starting at 0
- script.js: click increments count, second button resets to 0
- Link script with defer
- No frameworks

Explain querySelector, addEventListener, and textContent.

prompts.label: fetch() load JSON
prompts.text:
Create a small page that loads JSON with fetch.

- Fetch https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users/1
- Show name and email in the HTML after load
- Show a loading message and an error message if fetch fails
- Use async/await

Provide index.html and script.js.

prompts.label: Express Hello server
prompts.text:
Create the smallest Express server for a beginner.

- server.js: GET / returns Hello from Express
- package.json with express dependency and start script
- Steps: npm install, npm start, test in browser
- Add GET /api/time returning current ISO time as JSON

Use CommonJS or ESM consistently and say which you chose.

prompts.label: React component skeleton
prompts.text:
I'm starting with React. Scaffold a minimal app with Vite (npm create vite@latest).

- One App component showing a title and a button that increments a counter (useState)
- Explain folder structure after create
- List commands to install and run dev server

Keep one file change at a time if possible; focus on JSX and props concept in 2–3 sentences.

prompts.label: Form validation (vanilla)
prompts.text:
Build a sign-up form with client-side validation (no backend).

- Fields: username (min 3 chars), email, password (min 8 chars), confirm password
- On submit: prevent default, show inline error messages
- Clear errors when user fixes input
- Vanilla JS only

Provide HTML, CSS, and JS.

prompts.label: Todo list (vanilla JS)
prompts.text:
Create a simple todo list app for learning JavaScript.

- Input + Add button creates a list item
- Each item: checkbox (complete), delete button
- Completed items show strikethrough
- Store todos in an array in memory (localStorage optional bonus)

Full HTML/CSS/JS with short comments.

prompts.label: ES modules import/export
prompts.text:
Teach ES modules with a tiny example.

- math.js exports add and multiply functions
- main.js imports them and logs results
- index.html loads main.js as type="module"
- Explain named vs default export in one paragraph

Show exact file tree and how to run with a static server (npx serve or Live Server).

prompts.label: async/await delay demo
prompts.text:
Explain async/await with a beginner demo (Node or browser).

- Function fetchUser() that waits 1 second then returns { id: 1, name: 'Ada' }
- Main async function prints Loading..., then user, using try/catch
- Compare briefly with .then() chains

Provide runnable code and expected console output.

prompts.label: package.json npm scripts
prompts.text:
Explain package.json scripts for a beginner JS project.

- Example package.json with scripts: start, dev (nodemon or vite), test (placeholder echo)
- What npm run start does vs npx
- devDependencies vs dependencies with one example each
- .gitignore for node_modules

Use a realistic but minimal project name.

prompts.label: Fix closures inside loops
prompts.text:
I'm running async callbacks in a loop and every item logs the wrong index/value.
Explain the historical var closure pitfall and the modern fix with let or with an IIFE pattern.
Give a clean for...of + await example for sequential calls.
Note when Promise.all with map is appropriate instead.
End with a 3-item checklist to spot similar bugs in event listeners.

prompts.label: Sort arrays without mutating surprises
prompts.text:
Teach me array sort pitfalls in JavaScript for interview prep level beginners.
Show how sort() mutates in place and how to copy with slice or spread first.
Provide a numeric comparator for numbers and a localeCompare example for names.
Explain stable vs unstable sort at a high level.
Warn about comparing objects without a comparator.

prompts.label: AbortController for cancellable fetch
prompts.text:
I want a fetch that can be aborted when the user navigates away or clicks Cancel.
Walk through AbortController + signal wiring on fetch.
Demonstrate a timeout pattern using AbortSignal.timeout if available, with a fallback.
Show how to catch AbortError separately from network errors.
Keep the example under one small module with comments.

prompts.label: URL and URLSearchParams parsing
prompts.text:
Compare hand-rolling query strings vs using URL and URLSearchParams.
Build a tiny helper that reads current page query params in the browser and safely encodes a new link.
Explain why decodeURIComponent can be dangerous if misused.
Touch on base URL resolution with new URL(relative, base).
Give one Node.js example reading process.argv vs import.meta.url context briefly.

prompts.label: Intl for dates and numbers
prompts.text:
Show beginners how Intl.NumberFormat and Intl.DateTimeFormat improve presentation.
Examples: currency in EUR, compact notation for large dashboard counts, short weekday in a locale.
Note timezone pitfalls: what the API can and cannot infer.
Compare to manual string concatenation anti-patterns.
Include feature detection thoughts for older browsers at a high level.

prompts.label: Custom Error subclasses
prompts.text:
I throw plain strings sometimes and want to improve.
Demonstrate class ValidationError extends Error with a code field and correct prototype chain.
Explain why instanceof checks matter in catch blocks.
Show capturing cause in modern engines when wrapping errors.
Give a pattern for mapping errors to HTTP statuses without frameworks.

prompts.label: WeakMap for private memoization idea
prompts.text:
At a beginner-friendly depth, explain Map vs WeakMap and why keys must be objects.
Sketch a tiny memoizeByObject(fn) using WeakMap for per-object caches that don't leak memory.
Contrast with a global Map keyed by strings.
Call out when this pattern is overkill.
No heavy algorithm analysis—just intuition and a 15-line demo.

prompts.label: structuredClone for deep copies
prompts.text:
Teach structuredClone as a modern alternative to JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(obj)).
List what types clone well and what fails (functions, symbols, DOM nodes).
Compare shallow copy with spread for nested objects.
Mention Same-Transferable considerations only lightly.
Provide two micro-examples: nested plain object and Date preservation.

prompts.label: Event loop mental model cartoon
prompts.text:
Help me predict console order for setTimeout, Promise.then, and sync code.
Explain the call stack, task queue, and microtask queue in plain language.
Give a short puzzle and solution walkthrough.
Relate why await can reorder perceived execution.
Finish with debugging advice using breakpoints vs console.log spacing.

prompts.label: Module import maps in the browser
prompts.text:
I see import maps mentioned for using npm packages directly in browsers without bundlers.
Outline the idea, trade-offs, and a minimal index.html snippet with script type importmap.
Explain when beginners should still prefer Vite instead.
Note CORS considerations for module scripts.
List one security footgun about remote module URLs.
